data.title: The Effects of Milk and Milk Constituents on Postprandial Inflammation in Overweight Subjects
data.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to determine whether the addition of milk or the milk constituents calcium and protein to a high-fat breakfast affects the postprandial lipid and inflammatory response.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Body mass index (BMI) above 27 kg/m2

Exclusion Criteria:

* Smoking
* Diabetes mellitus
* Cardiovascular diseases
* Familial hypercholesterolemia or lipid lowering medication
* Severe conditions that might interfere with the study outcomes, such as COPD, asthma, rheumatoid ar

data.title: Evaluation of the Effect of a Food Supplementation With a Combined Food Supplement on Lipid Pattern, Indexes of Non-alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease and Systemic Inflammation in Healthy Subjects With Suboptimal LDL-C Levels: a Two-arm Double-blind, Placebo- Controlled, Randomized, Clinical Trial
data.brief_summary: Several controlled interventional studies have shown that there is a close correlation between cholesterol reduction and cardiovascular risk; in fact, reductions in serum levels of total cholesterol and low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C), obtained through lifestyle modification or specific drugs, result in reductions in the incidence of major coronary events. The effectiveness of these interventions has been demonstrated both in subjects in primary prevention and in subjects in secondar
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. Subjects agree to participate in the study and having dated and signed the informed consent form.
2. Subjects who have the capability to communicate, to make themselves understood, and to comply with the study's requirements.

endpoints: LDL-cholesterolemia reduction from baseline and between groups
